narrative:
DRIVER
GBP leads H4 CSM at 100 versus D1 at 0, a 100-point divergence that is the single most important signal in this brief, confirming a sharp intraday GBP reversal higher that contradicts the dominant D1 USD strength narrative. GBPUSD and GBPJPY are the direct expressions, with GBPJPY amplified by its 0.91 correlation to GBPUSD and NZDJPY cluster confirmation at 0.76. Counter-narrative: AUD D1 CSM reads 100 but H4 fades to 57, meaning AUDJPY long remains valid on D1 but H4 momentum at 75 on AUDJPY is the only AUD entry with current confirmation.

MOMENTUM
Gaining: AUDJPY CMP 60, H4 momentum 75 rising sharply, D1 strong buy intact
Fading: EURUSD CMP 42, H4 momentum 22 falling hard; GBPJPY CMP 43, H4 43 fading despite CSM reversal signal

WATCH
Fed speaker reaffirms delayed cuts, USDCHF extends H4 strong buy above current resistance, targeting continuation of the D1 fading USD paradox resolution
UK political headline deteriorates, GBPJPY reverses the H4 CSM spike and retests D1 neutral range
Silver stabilises after 4% drop, AUDUSD H4 momentum at 57 accelerates higher confirming commodity currency resilience

themes.theme: US Dollar strength from resilient economic data and hawkish Fed commentary
Fed policy normalization delayed with emphasis on inflation risks
Safe-haven demand limited despite geopolitical commentary
Equity market strength supporting risk-on sentiment
GBP weakness from UK political uncertainty
Commodity currencies under pressure from stronger USD
Trump-Xi trade optimism providing limited support to risk assets

key_observation: US Dollar experiencing broad-based strength driven by better-than-expected retail sales data and hawkish Fed communications signaling extended policy pause. Equity markets rallying on tech strength and strong economic data. Commodity currencies and GBP weakening against the USD backdrop.
watch: Fed rate cut expectations and timeline; UK political developments; Trump-Xi meeting outcomes; US inflation trajectory; equity market sustainability of gains; safe-haven demand shifts
